Tell成立的前提是，人们并不总是能够在每月1日支付租金，但可能更适合在整个月内以较小的金额支付租金。通过其灵活的租金平台，Till为租户创建了一个与他们每月现金流保持一致的定制付款时间表。直到估计，它可以帮助减少高达50%的驱逐。
Till首席执行官大卫·沙利文(David Sullivan)告诉TechCrunch：“我们努力了解这一时机，我们可以研究他们的费用负担，帮助他们平衡现在应该支付更多费用，还是在本月晚些时候支付更多费用。”
有了这笔资金，Till计划在更多的州努力让更多的房东加入进来，并进一步开发灵活的租金产品。为了让租房者使用这个平台，他们的房东必须已经在与Till合作。到目前为止，Till住在170处房产中，总共包括14个州的3万个单元。
Metaprop General合伙人Zak Shwarzman在一份声明中表示：“自从我们第一次了解Till以来，它能够弥合租客日益波动的收入和支出模式与房东更加严格的财务现实之间的差距，给我们留下了极其深刻的印象。”“由于新冠肺炎疫情和相关经济影响带来的不确定性仍在继续，看不到明显结束的迹象，房东找到新的、互惠互利的方式与租户合作，以降低滞纳金，最大限度地减少驱逐，并促进租户的长期财务健康，这一点比以往任何时候都更加重要。”
滞纳金因州和房东而异。有时，他们以固定费用或租金的一定比例的形式出现。不管怎样，他们都是惩罚性的。
“对于有现金流问题的租房者来说，这是一笔非常惩罚性的费用，”沙利文说。“即使租房者有能力住在这个单元里，租房者也会不堪重负，这使得他们的支付能力更具挑战性。”
虽然这一产品如今可能更具相关性，但在人们因新冠肺炎而面临严重的经济不安全之际，沙利文表示，这个问题并不是大流行所特有的。然而，新冠肺炎加剧了这个问题。
“在Covid之前，你每年有大约500亿美元的拖欠租金，租房者被收取大约50亿美元的滞纳金，”沙利文说。“这给租房者带来了负担。这导致正常年份有300万户家庭被驱逐。驱逐对少数族裔社区的影响不成比例。“。
然而，这种商业模式依赖于财务不安全，因为Till的目标客户是那些已经在努力支付每月租金的人。对于其灵活的租金产品，Till向按时付款的租户收取每月3美元的费用，如果他们不按时付款，则收取每月9美元的费用。Till还为不同利率的租户提供租赁贷款产品。
沙利文说：“我们希望创造双赢的结果。”“我们从根本上相信，当你让租房者成功时，房东也会成功。”
note_en:
Till was founded on the premise that people are not always able to pay their rent on the 1st of the month, but might be better suited to paying their rent in smaller payments throughout the month. Through its flexible rent platform, Till creates a customized payment schedule for renters that aligns with their monthly cash flow. Till estimates it can help cut evictions by as much as 50%.
 “We work to understand that timing and we can look at their expense loads to help them balance if they should be paying more now or more later in the month,” Till CEO David Sullivan told TechCrunch.
 With the funding, Till plans to work on getting more landlords on board across additional states and further develop the flexible rent product. In order for renters to use the platform, their landlords must already be working with Till. To date, Till is live at 170 properties that consist of 30,000 units in total across 14 states.
 “Since we first learned about Till, we have been extremely impressed by its ability to bridge the gap between the increasingly volatile income and expense patterns of renters and the more rigid financial realities of landlords,” Metaprop General Partner Zak Shwarzman said in a statement. “As the uncertainty wrought by the COVID-19 pandemic and related economic fallout continues with no clear end in sight, it’s more important than ever that landlords find new, mutually beneficial, ways to work with renters to reduce late fees, minimize evictions and foster renters’ long-term financial health.”
 Late fees vary by state and by landlord. Sometimes they come in the form of a flat fee or a percentage of your rent. Either way, they’re punitive.
 “It’s a very punitive fee against a renter having a cash flow issue,” Sullivan said. “Even if a renter has the ability to stay in the unit, renters then get overburdened with fees, which makes their ability to pay even more challenging.”
 While this product may have more relevance these days, during a time when people are facing severe economic insecurity as a result of COVID-19, Sullivan said this problem is not specific to the pandemic. Though, COVID-19 has exacerbated the issue.
 “Pre-Covid, you have about $50 billion of delinquent rent a year and renters being charged about $5 billion in late fees,” Sullivan said. “That creates a burden on renters. It leads to three million families being evicted in normal year. And evictions disproportionately impact minority communities.”
 The business model, however, relies on financial insecurity, as Till’s target customer is someone who already struggles to make their monthly rental payments. For its flexible rent product, Till charges renters $3 per month if they make all of their payments on time, and $9 per month if they don’t. Till also offers a rental loan product for renters with varying rates.
 “We want to create win-win outcomes,” Sullivan said. “We fundamentally believe that when you get renters to succeed, landlords succeed to.”
title: TIL筹集800万美元试图防止驱逐
title_en: Till raises $8 million to try to prevent evictions

(CNN)新西兰在该国部分地区重新引入了冠状病毒限制措施，此前该国在当地传播的新病例打破了102天的记录，此前该国没有记录到当地感染病例。
新西兰总理杰辛达·阿德恩(Jacinda Ardern)周二晚间确认了四例新的本地传播冠状病毒病例，并宣布新西兰人口最多的城市奥克兰将从周三中午开始暂时实施三级限制措施，为期三天。
根据新西兰卫生总干事Ashley Bloomfield博士的说法，所有四个病例都是在南奥克兰的一个家庭内发现的。他补充说，最近没有一例新病例是在新西兰以外旅行的。
布卢姆菲尔德博士说：我们一直在为那段时间做准备，现在就是时候了。他补充说，医疗系统已经做好了充分的准备。
阿德恩说，为了与我们的预防措施保持一致，我们将要求奥克兰人与我们一起迅速采取行动，从明天中午12点，也就是8月12日星期三，我们将把奥克兰转移到3级限制。
她补充说，这将使卫生官员有时间进行紧急接触者追踪和评估情况。
新的限制意味着包括餐馆、酒吧和非必需品商店在内的企业将不得不关闭。人们现在还将只被允许离开家进行必要的活动，如从杂货店领取补给。在奥克兰，超过10人的集会也将受到限制。
奥克兰的学校也将停课三天。除奥克兰外，新西兰其他地区将进入二级限制。在这些限制下，不能超过100人的团体聚集在一个地方。
社交距离也必须在酒店企业进行，而博物馆、图书馆和游泳池等公共场所如果符合公共卫生措施并确保一米的物理距离和记录保存，就可以开放。
奥克兰和新西兰各地的新限制措施将从周三中午开始实施，持续至少三天，直到周五午夜。
note_en:
(CNN)New Zealand has reintroduced  coronavirus restrictions in parts of the country after new locally transmitted cases broke the 102-day streak  the country had gone without recording a local infection.
 New Zealand's Prime Minister  Jacinda Ardern confirmed four new locally transmitted coronavirus cases on Tuesday night, and announced that New Zealand's most populous city, Auckland, will temporarily see level three restrictions introduced for three days starting from midday on Wednesday.
  All four of the cases were found within one household in South Auckland according to New Zealand's Director-General of Health Dr Ashley Bloomfield. He added that none of the new cases had recently traveled outside of New Zealand.
   "We have been preparing for that time, and that time is now," said Dr Bloomfield adding that the "health system is well prepared."
 "In line with our precautionary approach we will be asking Aucklanders to take swift actions with us, as of 12 noon tomorrow, Wednesday August 12, we will be moving Auckland to level 3 restrictions," said Ardern.
  She added that this will give health officials time to conduct urgent contact tracing and assess the situation.
 The new restrictions mean that businesses including restaurants, bars and non-essential shops will have to close. People will also now only be allowed to leave their homes to conduct essential activities such as picking up supplies from grocery stores. Gatherings over 10 people will also be restricted in Auckland.
  Schools in Auckland will also be closed for three days. Outside of Auckland, the rest of New Zealand will go into level two restrictions. Under the restrictions groups of no more than 100 people can gather in one place.
   Social distancing must also be practiced at hospitality businesses, while public venues such as museums, libraries and pools can open if they comply with public health measures and ensure one meter physical distancing and record keeping.
 The new restrictions across Auckland and New Zealand will take place from midday on Wednesday and last at least three days until midnight on Friday.
title: 新西兰102天来首例本地传播病例后恢复冠状病毒限制
title_en: New Zealand Reinstates Coronavirus Restrictions After First Locally-Transmitted Case in 102 Days
